Prompt.Text: What is cloud migration and why is it important?

PrimaryResponse.Text: Cloud migration is the process of moving digital assets—data, applications, and IT processes—from on-premises infrastructure to cloud-based environments. It is important because it reduces costs, improves scalability, enhances security, and enables remote access to resources.

Prompt.Text: Compare Google Drive and OneDrive for business use.

PrimaryResponse.Text: Google Drive integrates tightly with Google Workspace (Docs, Sheets, Slides) while OneDrive integrates with Microsoft 365 (Word, Excel, Teams). OneDrive offers better Windows integration and compliance features. Google Drive is preferred for collaboration-heavy teams already in the Google ecosystem.

Prompt.Text: Which one has better security features?

PrimaryResponse.Text: Both offer strong security. OneDrive has Azure AD integration, advanced compliance tools, and Data Loss Prevention policies. Google Drive has zero-knowledge encryption and Context-Aware Access. For enterprise compliance (HIPAA, SOC2), OneDrive/Microsoft 365 has a broader certification portfolio.

Prompt.Text: How can I automate file migration between cloud providers using APIs?

PrimaryResponse.Text: You can automate cloud-to-cloud file migration using the source provider's API (e.g., Google Drive API) to list and download files, then upload them via the destination API (e.g., Microsoft Graph API for OneDrive). Use OAuth 2.0 for authentication, implement rate limiting, and track progress with a job queue. Tools like CloudFuze handle this at scale with built-in error recovery.
